docs.title: Welcome to the Training Workx Help Center
docs.text: Here you can find everything you need to know about using your Virtual Reality training.

docs.title: Installing the Launcher
docs.text:
The Launcher is used to install and update the VR training software and to start the Spectator application.

You can install the Launcher on your own Windows computer if you want to run the training without using a training case.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
Before installing the Launcher, make sure you have the following:

  • A stable Wi-Fi network
  • A Windows computer (preferably a laptop)
  • A USB-C port to connect the VR headset
  • A supported VR headset (at least Meta Oculus Quest 2, preferred Meta Quest 3/3S)

The Wi-Fi network must allow both the headset and the computer to connect to each other.

docs.title: Download the Launcher
docs.text:
1. Go to the Training Workx portal
  2. Navigate to Administration → Downloads
  3. Download the Launcher Installer

You will also find your company access code on this page.

docs.title: Install the Launcher
docs.text:
1. Open the downloaded Launcher Installer.
  2. Follow the instructions shown on screen.
  3. The Launcher will install automatically.

Once installation is complete, you can start the Launcher from your computer.

docs.title: Enter the access code
docs.text:
The first time you open the Launcher, it will ask for an access code.

  1. Copy the code from the portal download page.
  2. Enter the code in the Launcher.
  3. Confirm.

The Launcher will now connect to your company account and automatically install the Spectator software.

docs.title: What the Launcher is used for
docs.text:
After installation, the Launcher can be used to:

  • Start the Spectator
  • Updating the VR headset
  • Updating the Spectator software
  • Manage training connections between headset and computer

docs.title: Adding your own devices
docs.text: If you want to use your own VR headset instead of one supplied by Training Workx, you must first register the device in the portal. After registering the device, you can install the training software using the Launcher.

docs.title: Important
docs.text: Your VR headset must be in Developer Mode for the installation to work.

docs.title: Registering a device
docs.text:
1. Log in to the Training Workx portal.
  2. Go to Training → Devices.
  3. Click Register own device.
  4. Add the new headset.

The headset will now appear in your device list.

docs.title: Installing the software
docs.text:
After registering the headset, you can install the training software.

  1. Turn on the VR headset.
  2. Connect the headset to your computer using a USB-C cable.
  3. Open the Launcher.
  4. Go to the Update section.
  5. Click Update to install the software.

The installation process may take a few minutes.

docs.title: Identifying the headset
docs.text:
When the installation is complete:

  1. Put on the VR headset.
  2. Start the training application.
  3. You will see a list of unregistered headsets.
  4. Select the correct name for your device.

The headset will now be linked to your account and ready to use for training.

docs.title: Updating your own headset
docs.text:
The VR headset can be updated using the Launcher on your computer. Keeping the headset up to date ensures you always have the latest training content and improvements.

You can update both the headset and the Spectator software from the Launcher.

docs.title: Update instructions
docs.text: Updating your own system works the same way as updating a Training Workx box. For more information, view the instructions here: Using Your Box > Updating Your Box.

docs.title: Selecting modules
docs.text: When you start the training, you always start in our training lobby. The lobby looks like an office space in Shanghai where you have a large screen in front of you. This menu allows you to select what module you want to play.

docs.title: 1. Selecting the training
docs.text:
If you're using a device with multiple trainings (such as Sofia VR, and Tom VR), you will first have to select which training you want to play. You do this by touching the training button with your hand.

If you later want to switch to a different training, you can do this by touching the training head on the top left of the screen with your hand.

docs.title: 2. Selecting the module
docs.text:
Once you've selected the training, you can now select which module you want to train. Select the module by touching it with your hand.

If you want to switch back to a different module, you can use the arrow on the bottom of the screen.

docs.title: 3. Selecting the training mode
docs.text: Most modules allow you to play a practice mode and a recognition game. After selecting a module, you will have to decide which of these you want to play. The practice module teaches the student something, while a recognition game or exam tests whether the student can do something by themselves.

docs.title: Playing a module
docs.text: Once you start playing a module, you will be brought to a different room. In this room, you can find a screen on the wall giving you instructions. These instructions will also be communicated through voice overs in the headset.

docs.title: Instruction screen
docs.text:
The instruction screen will tell you what to do in the training. When you get stuck, you can always move back to the screen to see what it says. It can also provide images to help point you in the right direction.

Some instruction screens also have a progress bar underneath the instruction. They will show you how far in the module you have progressed.

docs.title: Completing steps
docs.text: Instructions are usually also marked by highlights or arrows. By completing the right actions, the next instruction will automatically play and the instruction screen will change.

docs.title: Settings screen
docs.text:
If you want to open the settings screen, you do so by touching the gear on the top right of the instruction screen. This screen provides you with a couple different options:

  • Skip this step and move to the next step
  • Close the module and go back to the lobby
  • Replay the last voice over

docs.title: Using the Spectator
docs.text:
The Spectator allows a trainer to watch what is happening inside the VR headset and manage the training session.

With the Spectator you can follow the trainee live and control parts of the training from the computer.

docs.title: Starting the Spectator
docs.text:
1. Start the system and open the Launcher.
  2. Press the Start Spectator button.
  3. The Spectator application will open.

You will see a list of available VR headsets.

docs.title: Connecting to a headset
docs.text:
1. Select the headset you want to monitor.
  2. Press Confirm.

Once connected, the screen will show the live view from the VR headset.

docs.title: Managing the training
docs.text:
From the Spectator interface you can control different parts of the training.

For example you can:

  • Switch the language
  • Change the active student
  • Open training settings

This allows the trainer to guide the training session without interrupting the trainee.

In the lobby, you can also start modules.

In the module, you can also skip steps and close modules.

docs.title: Showing/hiding the controls
docs.text:
The interface buttons are visible in the lobby. While you are in a module, the interface buttons are hidden by default.

  • If you want to hide the interface buttons: Press the arrow in the top-left corner of the screen.
  • If you want to show the interface buttons: Simply tap anywhere on the screen.

docs.title: Changing settings
docs.text: By pressing the settings button, you can get a menu with a bunch of different spectator options. These are saved on the box, so you don't have to set them again every time you train.

docs.title: Switching students
docs.text:
During training it is possible to switch between different students. This allows multiple trainees to use the same VR headset while keeping their progress separate.

Students are managed through the online portal and can then be selected inside the training.

docs.title: Selecting a student in VR
docs.text:
1. Start the VR training.
  2. The training will begin in the Lobby.
  3. On the right side of the whiteboard you will see the student panel.
  4. Select the student you want to train.
  5. Touch Press to confirm

The selected student will now be active for the training session.

Keep in mind that changing student will change the language to their preferred language. The preferred language can be set in the training portal.

docs.title: Managing students as a trainer
docs.text:
Before students can be selected in VR, they must be added by the trainer.

  1. Go to the online portal.
  2. Add the students to the correct class or class.
  3. Select which class is training on the headset. See Setting up classes.

Once this is done, trainees can switch between students inside the training.

docs.title: Switching students from the Spectator
docs.text:
Trainers can also switch the active student using the Spectator.

This allows the trainer to change students without interrupting the trainee in VR.

docs.title: Changing the language
docs.text:
The VR training supports multiple languages. You can change the language before starting a training module. The spectator can also change language during the training.

The selected language will be used for the instructions, voice overs, and interface inside the training.

docs.title: Changing the language in VR
docs.text:
1. Start the VR training.
  2. You will begin in the Lobby.
  3. On the whiteboard, select the Change Language option.
  4. Choose the language you want to use.

The training will now continue in the selected language.

docs.title: Changing the language using the Spectator
docs.text:
Trainers can also change the language from the Spectator.

  1. Open the Spectator.
  2. Connect to the VR headset.
  3. Open the Settings > Language option in the controls.
  4. Choose if you want to change the Spectator or Student language (the Spectator is the language on the box, the student is the language in the headset).
  5. Select the language you want.

The language will update instantly.

docs.title: Setting up students
docs.text:
Before students can start training, they must first be created in the Training Workx portal. This allows the system to track training progress and results for each student.

You can access the portal at:

  • Logistic Workx: portal.logisticworkx.com
  • Cleaning Workx: portal.cleaningworkx.com

docs.title: 1. Create a student
docs.text:
1. Log in to the portal.
  2. Go to Training → Students.
  3. Click the New Student button.
  4. Enter the student details.

Each student must have a unique staff number. This number identifies the student during training.

docs.title: 2. Create a class
docs.text: Classes allow multiple students to use the same headset and switch between users. See Setting up classes.

docs.title: 3. Connect the headset to a class
docs.text:
To allow students to switch users inside the VR training, you must link a headset to a class.

  1. Go to Training → Devices.
  2. Select the VR headset you want to use.
  3. Choose the class that will train on that headset.

Once connected, students from that class will appear inside the VR training and can be selected during the session.

docs.title: 4. Switching students in VR
docs.text: After the headset is linked to a class, students can switch users inside the VR training from the student panel next to the module selection screen.

docs.title: Setting up classes
docs.text:
Classes allow multiple students to train using the same VR headset. Students within the same class can switch between users during training.

Classes are created and managed in the Training Workx portal.

You can access the portal at:

  • Logistic Workx: portal.logisticworkx.com
  • Cleaning Workx: portal.cleaningworkx.com

docs.title: Creating a class
docs.text:
1. Log in to the Training Workx portal.
  2. Go to Training → Classes.
  3. Click New Class.
  4. Enter a name for the class.
  5. Select the students that belong to this class.
  6. Save the class.

Choose a clear name so trainers can easily identify the correct class.

docs.title: Assigning a class to a headset
docs.text:
To allow students from a class to train on a headset, the headset must be linked to that class.

  1. Go to Training → Devices.
  2. Select the headset you want to use.
  3. Choose the class that will train on that headset.
  4. Save the changes.

docs.title: Switching students during training
docs.text:
Once a headset is linked to a class, students can switch users inside the VR training.

This can be done from the student panel next to the module selection screen.

docs.title: Viewing results
docs.text:
The Training Workx portal allows trainers to view the progress and results of each student.

Results show how often a student trained, which modules were completed, and the scores achieved.

You can access the portal at:

  • Logistic Workx: portal.logisticworkx.com
  • Cleaning Workx: portal.cleaningworkx.com

docs.title: Viewing student progress
docs.text:
1. Log in to the Training Workx portal.
  2. Go to Training → Students.
  3. Find the student you want to view.
  4. Click View Modules.

You will now see which modules the student has completed and their current progress.

docs.title: Viewing module results
docs.text:
To see the detailed results for a specific module:

  1. Open the student’s module list.
  2. Click View Results for the module.

The results page shows:

  • The number of times the module was played
  • The type of session (training or exam)
  • The score achieved
  • The student’s progress over time

docs.title: Exporting results
docs.text:
If needed, you can export the results for further analysis.

  1. Go to the Students page.
  2. Press the 3 dots next to the green New student button.
  3. Export the results as a .csv file.

This file can be opened in spreadsheet software for reporting or record keeping.

docs.title: Using lesson plans
docs.text:
Lesson plans allow trainers to organize which training modules students should complete. They make it easier to guide students through a structured training program.

Lesson plans are managed through the Training Workx portal.

You can access the portal at:

  • Logistic Workx: portal.logisticworkx.com
  • Cleaning Workx: portal.cleaningworkx.com

docs.title: Creating a lesson plan
docs.text:
1. Log in to the Training Workx portal.
  2. Go to Training → Lesson Plans.
  3. Click New Lesson Plan.
  4. Enter a name for the lesson plan.
  5. Select the modules you want to include.
  6. Save the lesson plan.

docs.title: Assigning a lesson plan
docs.text:
Once a lesson plan is created, it can be assigned to students or classes.

  1. Open the lesson plan in the portal.
  2. Select the students or class that should use it.
  3. Save the changes.

Students will now see the assigned modules when starting the VR training.

docs.title: Using lesson plans in VR
docs.text:
When students start the training, the modules from the lesson plan will appear in the module selection screen.

They can then complete the modules in the order defined by the trainer.

docs.title: My boundary is too small
docs.text: If the play area (Guardian) is incorrect or the headset has been moved to a new room, you may need to reset the play area. You can find the instructions for this here: Using Your Box > Guardian Setup.

docs.title: Restarting your headset
docs.text:
If the VR headset is not responding correctly, restarting it can often fix the issue. This will close all running applications and start the headset again.

Restarting does not remove any data or training progress.

docs.title: Restarting from the headset
docs.text:
1. Put on the VR headset.
  2. Press and hold the power button on the headset.
  3. A power menu will appear.
  4. Select Restart.
  5. Wait for the headset to restart.

The headset will automatically turn off and start again.

docs.title: Restarting if the headset is frozen
docs.text:
If the headset does not respond:

  1. Press and hold the power button for about 10 seconds.
  2. The headset will turn off.
  3. Press the power button again to turn it back on.

docs.title: No Wi-Fi connection
docs.text: If the Spectator or the headset shows a No Wi-Fi popup, it may be possible that the box doesn't have enough cellular reception. There are a couple ways you can prevent this.

docs.title: 1. Move the box to a different location
docs.text:
Moving the box to a location with better connectivity is usually the best choice. Avoid strong steel constructions and move the box closer to windows and the outside.

If this isn't an option, or there is still not enough coverage, you can try seeing if the box and headset are connected correctly.

docs.title: If the popup appears on the box
docs.text:
1. Close the training application.
  2. On the touchscreen of the case, look at the task bar in the bottom right corner.
  3. Press the Wi-Fi icon.
  4. Check if the system is connected to the VRCase network.

If it is not connected, select VRCase and connect to it.

docs.title: If the popup appears in the headset
docs.text:
1. Put on the VR headset.
  2. Press the Meta (Oculus) button on the controller to open the menu.
  3. Open Quick Settings.
  4. Select Wi-Fi.
  5. Make sure the headset is connected to the VRCase network.

If it is not connected, select VRCase and connect to it.

docs.title: 3. After reconnecting
docs.text:
Once both the case and the headset are connected to VRCase, start the training application again. The headset and Spectator should now connect normally.

If you still don't have Wi-Fi, you will need to connect the box and headset to your own network. You can do this by going to the settings explained above and selecting your own network instead.

docs.title: 4. Offline mode
docs.text:
If using your own network also isn't an option, you can try using the headset's offline mode. This will allow you to see what the user is doing without an internet connection.

Keep in mind that this is only a backup solution, and that this means you won't be able to connect to your students and results data when using offline mode.

You can force offline mode by selecting Settings on the bottom left and then Force offline mode on the right.

If you get stuck with persistent internet connection issues, please contact support@cleaningworkx.com and we will help you get it fixed.

docs.title: Controllers not working
docs.text: If the controllers are not responding in the VR headset, there can be a couple different causes. Follow the steps below to fix the issue.

docs.title: 1. Do you have the right controllers?
docs.text:
Sometimes, when you have multiple headsets, the controllers can get swapped. It's important to check if they are the correct controllers.

If you put on your headset, the correct controllers will start vibrating. It can help to test with other controllers if you're not 100% sure.

docs.title: 2. Are the batteries empty?
docs.text: Most controller issues are caused by empty batteries.

docs.title: Removing the battery cover
docs.text:
• Hold the controller in your hand.
  • On the side of the controller you will see a small white release button.
  • Press this white button.
  • The battery cover will pop out slightly.
  • Remove the cover from the controller.

docs.title: Replacing the battery
docs.text:
1. Remove the old battery.
  2. Insert a new AA battery.
  3. Place the battery cover back onto the controller until it clicks into place.

Repeat these steps for both controllers if necessary.

docs.title: Turn the controllers back on
docs.text:
After replacing the batteries:

  1. Press any button on the controller.
  2. Wait a few seconds for the controller to reconnect to the headset.

The controllers should now work normally in VR.

docs.title: If the controllers still do not respond
docs.text:
1. Restart the VR headset.
  2. Turn the controllers on again.

If the issue continues, check that the batteries are installed correctly.

docs.title: Need support?
docs.text: If you did not find the help you needed in this Help Center, we are happy to help you directly.

docs.title: Contact support
docs.text:
Do you need help, but can it wait a day? Please contact us by e-mail. We will get back to you within 24 hours.

  • Email: support@cleaningworkx.com

For urgent questions, please don't hesitate to call us.

  • Phone: +31 618990693

docs.title: What's in the box
docs.text:
Our Training Boxes contain everything needed to run and supervise a VR cleaning training session. Before starting the training, it is important to understand which components are included in the VR training case.

Below is an overview of all components located inside the case.

docs.title: Touchscreen
docs.text: The built-in touchscreen is used to control the system inside the case. From here you can start the training software and operate the spectator interface to monitor the VR headset.

docs.title: VR Headset
docs.text: The VR headset is used by the trainee to follow the cleaning training in virtual reality. It provides the immersive training environment and allows interaction with the training scenarios.

docs.title: Controllers
docs.text: The controllers are used by the trainee to interact with objects inside the VR training. Each controller has a wrist strap to prevent dropping them during use.

docs.title: HDMI Input
docs.text: An external monitor can be connected using the HDMI input. This allows other people in the room to watch the training session on a larger screen.

docs.title: Power Cable
docs.text: The power cable supplies electricity to the system. Once connected to a power outlet, the computer and touchscreen inside the case will start automatically.

docs.title: Hidden Components
docs.text: The box also contains some hidden components on the inside. You will never have to do anything with these, but it still might be good to know that they exist.

docs.title: Built-in Computer
docs.text: Inside the case is a computer that runs the VR Spectator software and manages the connection with the VR headset. This system allows trainers to monitor and control the training session.

docs.title: Wi-Fi Modem
docs.text:
Inside the case there is a built-in modem. This modem provides the internet connection needed for the system to work.

The modem uses mobile reception, so the case must be used in a location with sufficient signal.

If there is no connection or the training cannot connect to the headset, check the troubleshooting section at Troubleshooting – No Wi-Fi.

Understanding these components will make it easier to follow the rest of the setup instructions.

docs.title: Starting the box
docs.text: This box allows you to easily stay up to date, see what the trainee is doing in VR, and start training as quickly as possible. The process for starting the box is quite simple.

docs.title: 1. Plug in the box
docs.text: Plug the box into a wall socket. This will turn on the box and the screen automatically. Startup will take approximately 2 minutes.

docs.title: 2. Optional: Connect a screen
docs.text: If you want to, you can now connect a screen using the HDMI cable in the box. Simply plug the cable into the right corner of the box. The screen will automatically connect when you start the spectator.

docs.title: 3. Turn on the headset
docs.text: While you're waiting for the box to start, you can turn on the Virtual Reality headset by pressing and holding the power button on the side. The headset will take a couple of seconds to start up. You can then put on the headset.

docs.title: 4. Start the spectator
docs.text: Now that the box has started up, you can start the spectator by tapping the Start button on the screen of the box. Afterwards, you can select the headset that you want to watch.

docs.title: 5. Select headset
docs.text: After the spectator has started, you can now select the headset. Select the headset you want to watch and press Confirm.

docs.title: 6. Connecting
docs.text:
Your box will now automatically connect to the headset once it comes online. In the meantime, it will display a range of different images while it is loading.

The box is now fully set up and ready to use. All you need to do is put on the headset and start training.

For more information, see Using Your Box → How to Use VR.

docs.title: How to use the VR headset
docs.text: Before starting the training, the trainee needs to correctly use the VR headset and controllers. Follow the steps below to ensure a comfortable and safe VR experience.

docs.title: 1. Pick up the controllers
docs.text:
Before putting on the VR headset, first pick up the controllers.

  1. Hold one controller in each hand.
  2. Put your hands through the wrist straps.
  3. Tighten the straps so the controllers stay securely on your wrists.

This prevents the controllers from falling during the training.

docs.title: 2. Put on the VR headset
docs.text:
1. Turn the dial on the back of the headset to loosen the head strap.
  2. Place the headset on your head.
  3. Adjust the headset position until the image looks clear.
  4. Tighten the dial on the back until the headset sits comfortably.
  5. Adjust the top strap if necessary so the headset stays stable.

docs.title: 3. Adjust the image
docs.text:
For the best training experience the image should be sharp.

  • Move the headset slightly up or down on your face.
  • Adjust the straps if necessary.
  • Make sure the headset sits comfortably but firmly.

The sharpest position may differ for each person.

docs.title: 4. Ready to go!
docs.text:
Once the headset and controllers are set up correctly, you are ready to begin the VR training.

You always start by Using Your Box → Setting up the Boundary.

docs.title: Setting up the play area
docs.text:
Before starting the VR training, you must set up the play area. This is called the Guardian. The Guardian creates a virtual boundary that prevents the user from walking into walls or objects while wearing the headset.

The Guardian is automatically remembered by the headset, so you only need to set it up when you visit a new location.

docs.title: Recommended space
docs.text:
For the best experience we recommend creating a large enough guardian. Otherwise, you might not be able to reach everything. The recommended size depends on the training:

  • Sofia VR: 2.5 × 3.5 meters
  • Tom VR: 3.5 × 4.0 meters

Make sure there are no objects inside the play area, such as chairs, tables, or cleaning equipment.

docs.title: 1. Open quick settings
docs.text: Press the menu button on the right controller. Only press this button once, holding the button won't work. After the menu opens, select the Quick settings button on the bottom left. This will open the Quick Settings menu on your right.

docs.title: 2. Open boundary settings
docs.text:
In the Quick Settings menu, select the Boundary button with your controller.

You can then select Switch to Roomscale (or Adjust Roomscale if you're already in the right mode).

docs.title: 3. Select roomscale
docs.text:
Once you're in the guardian editor, you will now see a camera view of the real world. Here you can draw a new play area.

Always make sure you select Roomscale. Do not select Stationary.

After selecting Roomscale, the system will make a boundary for you. This boundary is usually too big, so it is better to then select Choose your own boundary. This allows you to draw your own play area.

docs.title: 4. Drawing the boundary
docs.text:
The system will first ask you to confirm the floor level. It will automatically decide the floor level. If this is correct, can you can press Confirm. Otherwise, you need to select Redo and move your hands to the floor to calibrate this yourself.

After confirming the floor level, you can then draw the boundary by yourself. You do this by pointing with your controller and using the trigger in your hand to draw on the floor.

docs.title: 6. Confirm and return to training
docs.text:
Once you have completed drawing the boundary, you can look around to see if it's correct. If it is correct, you can select Confirm. Otherwise, you have to select Redraw and draw again.

After the boundary is set up, you can now select the Resume button to go back to the training.

docs.title: Turning off the box
docs.text: Turning off the box is very easy. You need less than a minute to do it. Do make sure you don't forget putting everything back in the box. This prevents you from running into problems when starting up later on.

docs.title: 1. Take out the power cable
docs.text: Take the power cable of the box out of the power outlet and put it back in the box. This can always be done safely, and you don't need to worry about losing any data.

docs.title: 2. Turn off the headset
docs.text: Turn off the headset by pressing the power button on the side and holding it for a few seconds. It will play a sound and the lights will go off.

docs.title: 3. Pack up and leave
docs.text:
You can now put everything back in and close the box. Please be mindful of cables getting stuck between the lid. Make sure you tighten all 4 safety locks so the box doesn't accidentally open.

After you've closed the box, you are now ready to bring the box with you wherever you want.

docs.title: Updating your box
docs.text:
The system can be updated to receive the latest improvements and training content.

Updates are managed through the Launcher, which opens automatically when the box starts.

docs.title: Downloading the update
docs.text:
Before an update can be installed, the new version must first be downloaded. This download happens automatically in the background when the case is connected to the internet.

Depending on the internet connection, downloading the update can take up to one hour. You can only install the update after the download has finished.

docs.title: Installing the update
docs.text:
Once the update has finished downloading:

  1. Click on the Update button in the Launcher.
  2. Make sure the VR headset is turned on.
  3. Connect the headset to the case using the USB-C cable inside the case.
  1. Press Update.

The system will update both the VR headset and the Spectator software.

docs.title: Installation time
docs.text:
Installing the update usually takes around 5 minutes.

Make sure the headset stays connected during the update process.
